financial.note: Nathalin holds a 54.23% stake as of March 2026. In April 2026, Nathalin notified intent to submit a General Offer for shares, signaling strong internal backing and potential for delisting or further consolidation.
Holds ~3.39% stake; indicative of foreign institutional sentiment toward Thai mid-cap energy transport.

supply_chain.note: PRM provides long-term petroleum transport for PTT and its subsidiaries (OR/PTTGC). Revenue stability is heavily anchored in PTT's domestic fuel demand.
Primary vessel operator for Thai Oil's crude and refined product logistics. Beneficiary of TOP's 'Clean Fuel Project' (CFP) volumes.
Clients for Floating Storage and Offloading (FSO) and Offshore Support Vessels (OSV) like hybrid crew boats; growth driven by regional E&P activity.

equity_cross_holdings.entity: V.C. Shipping and Service Co., Ltd.
equity_cross_holdings.note: Acquisition finalized in late 2025/early 2026 to enhance Ship Agent and Shipping (SAS) services, contributing to 50% YoY revenue growth in that segment.

competitors.note: Competes in chemical and refined oil tanker services across domestic and regional ASEAN routes.
Rival in the domestic petroleum tanker segment; PRM maintains a scale advantage with VLCCs and FSU assets.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: Directly impacts PCT (Petroleum and Chemical Tanker) segment revenue; sensitivity to Thai GDP and tourism recovery (fuel demand).
Forecasted to decline to ~$62/barrel in 2026. Lower fuel costs (bunker oil) improve margins for transport operators if charter rates remain stable.
Major portion of revenue (FSU/OSV) is USD-linked; a weaker THB inflates translated earnings and asset valuations.
After a temporary dip in early 2026 (75%), utilization recovered to >90% by April, acting as a core EBITDA driver.
